investigation.verdict: GRADE C — no accessibility tree exists to map (design U4 STOP)
evidence: UIA dump: 7 nodes, all window chrome (TitleBar/MenuBar/Min/Restore/Close); zero content nodes
descendants() double-pass (a11y wake attempt): 6 elements, unchanged — no lazy Chromium tree
EnumChildWindows: ZERO child HWNDs under top-level 'Window Class' — no Chrome_RenderWidgetHostHWND / Chrome_WidgetWin / WebView2 host
Process: single KrakenDesktop.exe (C:/Program Files/Kraken Desktop), no renderer child processes, install dir is the bare exe
Conclusion: custom GPU-rendered UI (not Electron/WebView); no AccessKit/UIA provider responding beyond the native frame
investigation.s3_resolution: no accessibility activation flag applies — the app is not Chromium-based
investigation.consequence: BRIDGE-UIA write path (feature 3) DEAD as designed-for; per design U4 fall back to the vision-executor sketch as a NEW design round. Read-side reconcile aid pivots to screenshot/vision (Claude read-tier or VLM) instead of tree parsing.
